review_text: I grew up in Texas, where my favorite food was Tex-Mex.  Although that's fairly stereotypical, I think it says a lot for my point of view.  Anyway, this is by far the best Tex-Mex I've had in a long, long time.  It's top notch, it's fairly quick, and reasonably priced.  I have been here twice; two weeks in a row.  I love it!  The hot sauce on the table is actually hot, the chips are great, the enchiladas are wonderful as is the fajita taco salad.  Great place!

Honestly, not the best. Our waitress seemed really unenthused to be serving  us. The food itself was okay, but I did find a charred piece of meat in my vegatable fajitas. Which made me wonder what their kitchen must look like, hope its not riddled with cross contamination. You pay at the front of and I stood waiting for 5 of more mintues with another patron for someone to cash me out with no explanation as to why it took so long and no apology. The bathroom was also kind of gross. Overall the food was mediocre and the restaruant could have been more clean. I would not recommend this place. You'd probably get a better experience at the Taco Bell down the street to be honest.

review_text: Great authentic Mexican food

It has a festive atmosphere with decent sized comfortable booths and tables.  Prices are within the expected range.  It is  conveniently located with plenty of parking.  Wait staff are friendly and helpful.  The restaurant in general is clean and in good repair. Food was ok and portion sizes are generous.   The rice is delicious but other things are hit or miss.  The pepper for the Chile rellenos was over processed and wet on the inside like it was a frozen item that was just dropped into the fryer.  The guacamole tasted like it was pre-made and had started to turn.   Not a big fan of canned guac.  It was obvious that they use good quality cheese but it hadn't been warmed long enough to melt.  The beans were a little dry and flavorless like they had been scooped right out of a can.  The complimentary salsa is quite tasty and the chips came right away hot from the fryer. My husband said his meal was quite good.  He got what he always gets at Mexican style restaurants... An enormous beef wet burrito.  Not sure exactly what the item was called, but it was the lunch special.  Overall it seems to be a wierd mix between restaurant and cafeteria quality food/cooking.  My recommendation is if you go,  bring a coupon so you feel like you got a deal.  Have them substitute rice for beans in your order.  Make sure you have a fresh round of salsa when your meal comes because it is delicious on everything.   Pass on the guacamole and avoid mid afternoon when I am pretty sure they have the housekeeping staff cooking.
